world.environment: remote jungle research camp / river outpost
world.cultural_context: International team of researchers, indigenous community, and suspected traffickers in a remote location. Satellite communication is limited. The stolen artifact has deep cultural significance to the local indigenous community and commercial value to the international art market.
physics_constraints: No reliable cell signal — satellite phone only
Jungle terrain limits movement; nearest town is hours by boat
No law enforcement presence — investigation must be conducted by the group

expected_factions: Art Trafficking Network (background — one or more suspects are connected)
character_archetype_hints: lead archaeologist (suspect — knows the artifact's value)
boat captain / logistics person (suspect — could have enabled extraction)
key_clue_type_hints: physical: disturbed soil or vegetation showing movement through specific jungle paths
digital: satellite phone records or camera metadata
documentary: shipping manifests or contact list found in personal items
environmental: footprints, water disturbance, weight marks inconsistent with stated alibis
testimonial: indigenous guide's knowledge of who was where — trigger: ask in their own language
interrogation_mechanic_notes: Cultural and language barriers complicate interrogation. Indigenous characters may reveal information only under specific respectful conditions. Researchers may be protective of the expedition's reputation.
generation_notes: The 'Amazonia' setting should use jungle terrain actively — not just as backdrop. At least one clue should require physical tracking through the environment. The cultural property angle (who has rightful claim to the artifact) should complicate motive.
